steps.title: Identify the measuring barrier
steps.content: The passage explains that inaccurate population counts make it impossible to determine whether the population is increasing, decreasing, or staying stable.

steps.title: Apply this to the study's purpose
steps.content: The study evaluates conservation strategies but cannot reliably measure population changes. Without this measurement, it risks drawing inaccurate conclusions about whether the strategies are effective. A, C, and D address unrelated concerns. That matches choice B — B is correct.
